commit.message:
Add full finance toolkit — 7 tools in one app

- app.py: home page with tool overview
- pages/1: 5-Year Financial Model (moved from root)
- pages/2: Annual Budget (existing)
- pages/3: Lending Calculator (loan + mortgage with early repayment)
- pages/4: Portfolio Analysis (Sharpe, VaR, risk/return scatter)
- pages/5: Stock Comparison (2 stocks, correlation, ratios)
- pages/6: Commodities Dashboard (gold, silver, oil, gas, copper, wheat)
- pages/7: Stock Analysis (any ticker, MAs, volatility, company info)

All charts rebuilt in Plotly. Consistent styling across all pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix pivoting-agent lint: put search_skills/list_skills on same line as "do not"

The lint test checks per-line for search_skills()/list_skills() references
and only exempts lines containing "do not" or "never call". The prohibition
was split across two lines, so the line with the function names didn't match.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add pivot-aware scanning to network-recon, improve dashboard agent lifecycle

network-recon skill:
- Add "Pivot Mode" section with two-phase scanning approach
- Phase 1: fast host discovery on pivot host (ARP, ping sweep, DNS zone,
  single-port proxychains sweep)
- Phase 2: targeted nmap through proxychains on known-live hosts only
- Document nmap MCP server limitation (no proxychains support)
- Add static nmap alternative for speed-critical scenarios
- Add pivot-specific troubleshooting entries

Dashboard (tail-agent.py):
- Auto-discover new agents from tasks directory (no .dashboard file needed)
- Red *stopped* indicator in pane header for dead agents
- Detect agent completion via JSONL final message + 5s mtime debounce
- Detect killed agents (TaskStop) via 30s mtime staleness
- Keep stopped agents visible instead of auto-removing
- Space bar toggles agents in/out from browser overlay

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

style: redesign navbar icons and mobile tab-bar layout

Redraw all four SVGs for clarity at small sizes: hardcover
cookbook with curved pages and visible binding, Thai mortar
and pestle, sharp-cornered restaurant menu card, classic
shopping cart. Bump icon size from 1rem to 1.15rem with
heavier 1.8 stroke weight. Mobile view switches from
hidden labels to a tab-bar layout with icons above tiny
labels, matching familiar mobile app patterns.

Add network pivoting support: tunnels table, pivoting-agent, interim writes

- Add tunnels table to state.db (schema v2→v3) with tunnel_type,
  pivot_host, target_subnet, local_endpoint, requires_proxychains, status
- Add tunnel MCP tools: get_tunnels (all modes), add_tunnel (interim+writer),
  update_tunnel (writer only); include Tunnels section in get_state_summary()
- Remove CHECK constraint from state_events.event_type to allow 'tunnel' events
- Create pivoting-agent (sonnet, skill-router + shell-server + state-interim)
  for tunnel setup via pivoting-tunneling skill with sudo handoff protocol
- Update all 5 discovery skills to use state-interim MCP with interim writes
  (add_credential, add_vuln, add_pivot, add_blocked) for real-time findings
- Add pivoting workflow to orchestrator: pivot-to-tunnel flow, tunnel context
  in agent prompts, tunnel health checks, pivoting-agent routing
- Update docs: CLAUDE.md, state-server README, orchestrator routing tables

commit.message:
fix: upgrade Gramine base image from v1.5 to v1.9 (#15)

* fix: upgrade Gramine base image from v1.5 to v1.9

Upgrade the Docker base image from gramineproject/gramine:v1.5 to
gramineproject/gramine:1.9-jammy to address CVE-2024-25371 (CVSS 7.5
HIGH, "Sigy" signal injection attack) and multiple bundled library CVEs
in glibc, mbedTLS, and libcurl.

Changes:
- Update Dockerfile FROM to gramine:1.9-jammy with pinned digest
- Remove deprecated loader.entrypoint from manifest template (optional
  since Gramine v1.8, not present in v1.9 examples)
- Remove gramine.libos from sgx.trusted_files (auto-managed when
  loader.entrypoint is not set)
- Update README prerequisites to reflect v1.9 requirement
- Fix stale Rust version comment in Dockerfile (1.80 → 1.85)

Closes #14

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review comments

- Remove build-essential version pin from Dockerfile, relying on the
  pinned image digest for reproducibility
- Update README Rust version from 1.82.0 to 1.85.0 to match Dockerfile

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: pin build-essential to jammy version, remove insecure eventfd flag

- Pin build-essential to 12.9ubuntu3 (the version available in the
  1.9-jammy base image) for reproducible builds
- Remove sys.insecure__allow_eventfd = true from manifest; since
  Gramine v1.7, secure in-enclave eventfd is the default and the
  insecure host-based fallback widens the attack surface unnecessarily

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Fix shell syntax error in Jitsi debconf preseed command

The cert_preseed string contains parentheses in "certificate
(oportunity to obtain a Let's Encrypt certificate later)" which
broke when sudo_wrap escaped single quotes — the parentheses ended
up unquoted and sh interpreted them as a subshell. Switched the echo
from single quotes to double quotes so parentheses stay literal
inside the sudo_wrap sh -c '...' wrapper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(hub): prevent confirm token consumed before rate-limit/slot checks (#869)

The confirm token was `.remove()`d (consumed) before checking rate
limit and trade slot availability. If either check failed, the token
was gone but the frontend still held it — retrying gave "invalid or
expired confirm token".

Reorder: check rate limit → acquire trade slot → consume token.
If token validation fails after slot acquisition, release the slot.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 8 issues from Two Sigma review

Alpha / Model:
- Remove drift: 5-bar OLS μ on 1-min BTC returns has t-stat ≈ 0.1 (noise).
  Set μ=0 for pure GBM from current price — more stable, unbiased.
- Model-only SL: should_exit uses model fair_p (not crowd-blended) to check SL.
  Crowd lags on adverse moves; blending was masking broken thesis and
  preventing timely stops.
- Vol blend weights 5/20/75 → 15/45/40: _vols entries overlap 4/5 so
  effective long-run n ≈ 19 (not 96); corrects overclaimed 75% weight.

Execution:
- REPRICE_THRESHOLD 1¢ → 3¢: reduces cancel/replace frequency and
  information leakage to market makers.
- Edge recheck before reprice: if edge evaporated between compute_signal
  and reprice, skip the new order rather than placing at stale fair_p.
- Fill latency logged on every fill (signal_ts → fill_ts ms) to measure
  signal decay and identify adverse selection.

Risk:
- Drawdown circuit breaker: hard pause when session PnL < −10% of balance.
  Tracks cumulative PnL across expired markets + live positions.

Alpha expansion:
- Added KXETH15M to TRADE_SERIES; added ethusdt@kline_1m to Binance feed.
  ETH/BTC correlation ~0.7; independent Kalshi books = independent alpha.

commit.message:
refactor: replace warning() with debug_log() for consistent logging

Replace warning() calls with debug_log() in non-critical paths for:
- ml_ensemble.R: Model loading warnings -> debug_log
- ml_feature_engineering.R: Unknown type warning -> debug_log
- template_versioning.R: Version file load error -> debug_log
- ui_sidebar.R: Translation warnings -> debug_log

Using debug_log() provides:
- Centralized logging configuration
- Consistent log level control
- No user-facing warning noise in production

commit.message:
fix: disable React Compiler + add Netlify config

React Compiler (reactCompiler: true) conflicts with Framer Motion —
it over-optimizes useEffect hooks preventing animations from starting,
causing components to stay at opacity:0 on cold start (incognito mode).

Also adds netlify.toml with @netlify/plugin-nextjs for proper Next.js
server-side rendering support on Netlify.

commit.message:
Add CI workflow for automated testing and checks (#6)

## Motivation

Ensure code quality and standards

## Summary
This PR introduces a GitHub Actions CI workflow to automate testing and
code quality checks on every push and pull request.

## Key Changes
- Added `.github/workflows/ci.yml` workflow configuration that:
  - Runs on Ubuntu latest environment
  - Sets up Node.js with LTS version
  - Installs dependencies using `npm ci`
  - Executes test suite via `npm test`
  - Runs code checks via `npm run check`
  - Leverages npm caching for faster builds

## Details
The workflow ensures consistent code quality and test coverage by
automatically validating all commits and pull requests against the
project's test and check scripts before merging.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BxDrJa8iExbJJ1pt6Aobn8
